title: Shared definitions for the Levels of Interoperability framework
description: Reusable type definitions and controlled vocabularies referenced by the four data schemas.

resourceId.description: Identifier for a resource type, of the form R followed by two or three digits.

levelId.description: Identifier for a treatment level, of the form T followed by two or three digits, optionally followed by a sublevel letter and an optional plus marker.

serviceId.description: Identifier for a tool or service, of the form S followed by two or three digits.

transitionId.description: Identifier for a transition, of the form X followed by two or three digits.

vocabularyTermId.description: Identifier for a vocabulary term, of the form V followed by two or three digits.

orderValue.description: Presentation order, independent of identifier. Gaps recommended to allow insertion without renumbering.

relatedResource.description: A reference to an external resource related to a framework entry.

type.description: The kind of relationship between the entry and the external resource.

identifier.description: The identifier of the external resource. May be a DOI, URL, ORCID, ROR, or other PID.

title.description: Human-readable title or label for the external resource.

note.description: Optional context about why this resource is related.

currentStatus.description: The development status of a tool, service, or reference resource.

scopeValue.description: A scope at which a level or attribute applies.

accessibilityValue.description: An accessibility state for a resource.

licensingValue.description: A re-use licensing state for a resource.

populationCompleteness.description: How fully populated a schema-bearing resource is, indicating progress within a level.

categoryValue.description: The category of a tool or service entry.
